name: South Dakota
examName: South Dakota Real Estate Broker Associate Examination

examFacts.notes: Administered by PSI for the South Dakota Real Estate Commission.

intro:
Roughly a third of South Dakota's real estate exam is state law, a 52-question portion you must pass on its own at 75%. The South Dakota Real Estate Commission contracts with PSI to deliver it as a combined exam, split into a 90-question national portion and a 52-question South Dakota state portion, and you have to clear 75% on each one separately. You sit for 150 minutes on the national questions and another 120 minutes on state law, which means roughly a third of everything you are graded on is South Dakota law. Passing comes down to how well you know the rules the Commission actually enforces, and that takes South Dakota-specific prep, not just national principles.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. South Dakota tests you on the duties and disciplinary powers of the Real Estate Commission, the brokerage relationships and agency disclosures spelled out in state law, how a licensee must handle trust accounts and earnest money, the seller's property condition disclosure statement, and the licensing rules in SDCL chapter 36-21A and the Commission's administrative code. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need South Dakota-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free South Dakota real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full South Dakota bank. Each one tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real SDREC practice test, and the ones you miss show you exactly what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the South Dakota question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the South Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: The South Dakota broker associate exam has 142 scored multiple-choice questions, split into a 90-question national portion and a 52-question South Dakota state portion. PSI may also add a handful of unscored experimental questions that don't count toward your result. The two portions are graded separately, so you have to pass both.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the South Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: You need a scaled score of 75% on each portion. The national and state sections are scored independently, so a strong national score won't rescue a weak state score. If you pass one portion and fail the other, you only have to retake the section you failed, not the whole exam.

faq.q: How much does the South Dakota real estate exam cost?
faq.a: The South Dakota Real Estate Commission lists the registration fee at $105, paid to PSI when you schedule your appointment. The fee is not refundable or transferable, and it covers both the national and state portions of the combined broker associate exam.

faq.q: How long is the South Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: You get 270 minutes total, broken into 150 minutes for the 90 national questions and 120 minutes for the 52 South Dakota questions. That is a four-and-a-half-hour testing block, so pacing matters. PSI delivers it on computer at test centers in Sioux Falls, Rapid City, and Madison, and your score is shown on screen before you leave.

faq.q: Who administers the South Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: The South Dakota Real Estate Broker Associate Examination is administered by PSI for the South Dakota Real Estate Commission. You register and schedule your exam date through PSI.

faq.q: Is the South Dakota real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It has a reputation for being demanding, mostly because the South Dakota state portion is dense with statute-specific detail and you have to clear 75% on it on its own. The questions aren't tricky for the sake of being tricky. They are written to check whether you actually understand South Dakota real estate law and the duties the Commission enforces. Most people who fail didn't slack off. They over-prepared on national principles and underestimated how much South Dakota-specific material is on the test. Honest practice on South Dakota-specific questions is usually the difference between passing on the first try and paying to retake a portion.

faq.q: What's on the South Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: The 90-question national portion covers contracts (about 19%), agency (about 13%), practice of real estate (about 12%), property ownership (about 10%), financing (about 9%), valuation (about 8%), property disclosures (about 7%), and smaller shares for transfer of title, land use controls, property management, and real estate math. The 52-question South Dakota portion breaks down into the duties and powers of the Real Estate Commission, licensing requirements, and the statutory rules governing how licensees operate, with that last group making up the bulk of the state questions.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the South Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish your 116-hour pre-license course, the highest-leverage thing you can do is grind through South Dakota-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. Generic national practice tests help with principles like contracts and financing, but they won't catch you on South Dakota's Commission rules, agency disclosures, and trust account requirements. The RealReady app gives you the full bank of South Dakota questions, plus progress tracking and a missed-question mode so you can drill what you're weak on. Most users study 30 to 60 minutes a day for two to four weeks before sitting the exam.

The 20 South Dakota practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the South Dakota bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the South Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated South Dakota question bank built for the South Dakota real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the South Dakota law that trips people up, like the brokerage relationships and agency disclosures spelled out in state law, the seller's property condition disclosure statement, and the licensing rules in SDCL chapter 36-21A.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward South Dakota's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
